references.title: AI Coding Agents 2026: Claude Code vs Cursor vs Kiro vs Copilot vs Windsurf — Pricing & Features Compared
Best AI Coding Tools 2026: Complete Ranking by Real-World Performance
Best AI Coding Assistants (2026): Cursor vs Copilot vs Claude Code & More

en.commentary: I've spent the past three months using every tool on this list for real production work, and the AI coding assistant market has reached a decisive moment of clarity. Claude Code is #1, and it's the right call. The 1M-token context window backed by Claude Opus 4.6's 80.8% SWE-bench score means it can reason coherently across an entire large codebase, holding the whole project's architecture, dependencies, and intent in context simultaneously. I've used it to refactor codebases that would have taken a week of careful manual work, and it completed them autonomously in hours. The terminal-first UX is more spartan than Cursor, and that's a real trade-off. The raw autonomous capability, though, is in a different league. Cursor at #2 is the best answer for developers who want AI deeply integrated into their IDE workflow for interactive development. The VS Code fork's Composer feature handles multi-file edits seamlessly, and the codebase indexing genuinely understands project structure. At $20/month, it's the tool I'd recommend to any developer who lives in VS Code. GitHub Copilot at #3 is where pragmatism lives. 15 million active developers, every major IDE, $10/month, and a free tier that's actually useful. Agent Mode is closing the capability gap. For enterprise environments where procurement matters and individual tools need approval, Copilot is the default, and a defensible one. Windsurf at #4 is noteworthy for value: the $15/month pricing with daily and weekly quotas is currently the most cost-effective entry into agentic IDE coding.

highlights.title: Claude Code Is #1 Because a 1M-Token Context Window Changes What 'Understands Your Codebase' Actually Means
highlights.body: Claude Code's 1M-token window, backed by Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 model, means it can hold your entire large project in context simultaneously. I've watched it trace a bug through 15 interconnected files, understand the architectural intent of a decade-old codebase, and propose refactors that respect patterns established in files it had accessed only through project context. That capability is truly transformative, a genuine paradigm shift in what autonomous coding assistance can deliver.

highlights.title: Cursor Is the Best IDE Experience in This Entire Category
highlights.body: Cursor's Composer feature handles multi-file editing with a coherence that leads this category of IDE-integrated tools. The codebase indexing builds a genuine semantic understanding of your project structure, reaching far beyond surface-level keyword search. I use Claude Code for autonomous tasks and Cursor for interactive development. Both are essential tools for a serious developer, and they cover different parts of the workflow.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot at $10/Month Is the Best Argument for 'Good Enough at the Right Price'
highlights.body: At $10/month with a free tier for students and open-source contributors, Copilot serves 15 million developers who need solid AI assistance with broad IDE compatibility and minimal setup. Agent Mode's continued improvement is closing the capability gap with Cursor for everyday tasks. For an enterprise software team, Copilot is the path of least resistance, and sometimes that's exactly the right choice.

highlights.title: Windsurf at $15/Month Is the Best Value Entry Into Agentic IDE Coding
highlights.body: Windsurf's combination of agentic features, clean UX, and $15/month pricing makes it the most accessible entry point into the category of AI tools that can actually plan and execute multi-step coding tasks independently. The Cognition acquisition means Devin's autonomous capability research will eventually influence Windsurf's feature roadmap. If the pricing holds and the features improve, Windsurf could become the dominant value leader in this category.

highlights.title: Devin at $20/Month Is the Most Interesting Bet on the Future of Autonomous Software Engineering
highlights.body: Devin was the world's first fully autonomous AI software engineer, and at $20/month (down from $500), it's now accessible to individual developers. The caveat: ACU-based usage costs ($2.25/15 min of compute) add up fast for iterative tasks. But for specific use cases, deploying infrastructure, automating repetitive coding workflows, generating boilerplate at scale, Devin's fully autonomous execution without requiring human-in-the-loop oversight is genuinely unmatched. This is what AI-assisted development looks like in 2027.

Cursor 拿第二，是希望 AI 深度嵌入 IDE 工作流、以互動開發為主的開發者最好的選擇。GitHub Copilot 第三，實用主義的代表：1500 萬活躍開發者、所有主流 IDE、月費台幣三百出頭，加上真正實用的免費版。Windsurf 第四，月費台幣約五百，目前進入代理 IDE 開發最划算的入口。

references.title: AI Coding Assistants in March 2026: What's New
Claude Code vs Cursor vs GitHub Copilot: The 2026 AI Coding Tool Showdown
Using AI to code does not mean your code is more secure

en.commentary: March 31st delivers the largest single-day ranking change this list has ever seen, and it's entirely Windsurf's own doing. Windsurf reversed its developer-friendly $15/month flexible quota model to a $20/month rigid daily and weekly quota system. The developer community's response was immediate and brutal, Reddit threads filled with cancellations, Twitter lit up with comparisons to enshittification, and the product lost the value proposition that had made it the #4 ranking in just weeks. I dropped the value score from 8.8 to 5.8, and the overall rank from #4 to #9. The underlying product is unchanged, the 8.8 IDE integration score and 8.2 code quality score remain valid, but a tool that penalizes heavy users with daily quota failures belongs outside the recommendations I make to developers who use AI coding assistants as core productivity infrastructure. Claude Code ends Q1 at its highest score: 8.8 overall, 9.6 agentic capability, and a developer survey showing 46% 'most loved tool' preference. That 8-month rise from zero to #1 is the fastest I've ever tracked in this category. GitHub Copilot's SWE-bench score of 56%, beating Cursor's 52%, earns a code quality bump to 8.6. Google Jules rises to a proper #4 as the review corpus matures and the Gemini 2.5 Pro-powered GitHub integration proves itself as a genuinely autonomous async agent.

highlights.title: Windsurf's Pricing Reversal Is a Trust Violation Pure and Simple, and It Costs Them 5 Ranking Places
highlights.body: Developers chose Windsurf at $15/month with flexible quotas because it represented an honest value exchange: pay a fair price, use what you need. Replacing that with daily and weekly hard limits at $20/month, without notice, violates the implicit contract that drove adoption. The value score drops from 8.8 to 5.8, and the rank drops from #4 to #9. If Windsurf reverses this decision, the scores come back up. Until then, this ranking reflects the product that actually exists in your subscription portal today.

highlights.title: Claude Code's 46% 'Most Loved' Rating After 8 Months Is Unprecedented in This Category
highlights.body: Developer surveys reveal functional truth in ways marketing surveys conceal. Developers who have tried multiple tools and converge on one have usually done so for functional reasons. Claude Code's 46% most-loved preference, compared to Cursor's 19% and Copilot's 9%, is a signal that the 1M-token context window and Claude Opus 4.6's reasoning capability have created something developers actively seek out and choose as their primary tool. The agentic capability score rises to 9.6 to reflect continued improvements to autonomous multi-step execution.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot's 56% SWE-Bench Score Beats Cursor, The Capability Gap Is Narrowing Fast
highlights.body: SWE-bench measures the ability to resolve real GitHub issues autonomously. Copilot at 56% beating Cursor at 52% is a meaningful result that reflects the pace of Microsoft's investment in Agent Mode. I've raised Copilot's code quality score to 8.6 accordingly. The pragmatic conclusion: GitHub Copilot has earned its place as a genuinely competitive tool on code quality metrics, and at $10/month with enterprise deployment advantages, the case for Copilot in large organizations has never been stronger.

highlights.title: Google Jules Earns Its #4 Ranking, with Gemini 2.5 Pro in a GitHub-Native Async Agent Providing a Compelling Proposition
highlights.body: Jules operates on a distinct model within this ranking: zero IDE dependency required, GitHub-native execution, cloud sandbox isolation, and async task planning with Gemini 2.5 Pro. The agentic capability score rises to 8.2 as real-world reviews confirm that Jules handles multi-file refactoring tasks with accurate project structure understanding. For teams who want autonomous coding tasks executed directly against their GitHub repositories with full cloud isolation, Jules is the most friction-free path.

highlights.title: 74 CVEs Linked to AI-Generated Code, Security Scanning Must Now Be a Core Evaluation Criterion
highlights.body: The Register's March 26th report confirming 74 CVEs attributable to AI-generated code is the most important security story in this category for Q1 2026. AI coding assistants require explicit security awareness. They generate code that compiles and passes tests but may contain injection vulnerabilities, insecure credential handling, or race conditions, the kinds of issues human reviewers are trained to catch. Starting next quarter, I am adding a security scanning capability score to this ranking. Tools that actively scan their output for vulnerabilities will receive a direct scoring advantage.

zh-tw.commentary:
3 月 31 日，這份排名有史以來最大的單日排名變動，完全是 Windsurf 自己造成的。他們把原本對開發者友好的 $15/月彈性配額模式，改成 $20/月加上嚴格每日、每週配額上限。開發者社群的反應很直接，Reddit 一堆取消訂閱文，Twitter 上到處是「enshittification」的比較，幾週內靠 CP 值衝上第四的產品，一夜之間失去了核心賣點。我把性價比評分從 8.8 降到 5.8，整體排名從第四掉到第九。底層產品本身沒變，8.8 的 IDE 整合和 8.2 的程式碼品質還是有效的，但一個拿每日配額懲罰重度用戶的工具，已經從我推薦給把 AI 工具當核心生產力基礎的開發者的名單裡移掉了。

Claude Code 以 8.8 的最高分結束 Q1，開發者調查顯示 46% 的「最愛工具」偏好。GitHub Copilot SWE-bench 56%，超過 Cursor 的 52%，程式碼品質評分提升到 8.6。Google Jules 隨評測資料成熟升到正式第四名。

highlights.title: Windsurf 偷改定價條件，這個決定讓他們掉了五個排名
highlights.body: 開發者當初選 Windsurf，就是衝著 $15/月彈性配額，這是誠實的價值交換：付合理的錢、按需求使用。改成 $20/月加嚴格每日和每週上限、事前沒有通知，這破壞了當初吸引用戶的隱性承諾。性價比評分從 8.8 降到 5.8，排名從第四掉到第九。如果 Windsurf 撤回這個決定，分數會跟著回升。在那之前，這個排名反映的就是你現在訂閱頁面裡實際存在的產品。

highlights.title: Claude Code 上線八個月拿到 46% 的「最愛工具」評分，這個品類前所未見
highlights.body: 開發者調查呈現的是功能層面的真相，用過多款工具最後集中到一款的人，通常是有具體理由的。Claude Code 46% 的最愛偏好，對比 Cursor 的 19% 和 Copilot 的 9%，說明 1M token 上下文窗口加上 Claude Opus 4.6 的推理能力，確實做出了開發者主動選擇放在工具鏈第一位的東西。代理能力評分升到 9.6，反映自主多步驟執行的持續進步。

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot SWE-bench 56% 超過 Cursor，能力差距在快速縮小
highlights.body: SWE-bench 測的是自主解決真實 GitHub Issues 的能力。Copilot 以 56% 超過 Cursor 的 52%，這是 Microsoft 持續投資 Agent Mode 的具體成果。我把 Copilot 的程式碼品質評分相應提升到 8.6。講實在的，Copilot 在程式碼品質指標上已經是真正有競爭力的工具，加上月費台幣三百出頭、企業採購優勢，大型組織選它的理由比以前更充分。

highlights.title: Google Jules 拿到第四名，Gemini 2.5 Pro 加 GitHub 原生非同步代理，這個組合說服力夠強
highlights.body: Jules 的模式跟清單上其他所有工具根本上就不同：完全零 IDE 依賴、GitHub 原生執行、雲端沙盒隔離、Gemini 2.5 Pro 驅動的非同步任務規劃。代理能力評分升到 8.2，因為真實評測確認 Jules 可以處理多文件重構任務，專案結構的幻覺問題比早期明顯減少。對於在台灣跑新創或小型開發團隊、想直接對 GitHub 倉庫下自主任務的人，Jules 是摩擦最少的路徑。

highlights.title: 74 個 CVE 跟 AI 生成代碼有關，安全掃描必須列入核心評估標準
highlights.body: The Register 3 月 26 日的報告確認 74 個 CVE 可歸因於 AI 生成的代碼，這是 2026 年 Q1 這個品類最重要的安全消息。AI 程式助理生成的代碼可以編譯、可以通過測試，但可能帶著注入漏洞、不安全的憑證處理、或是 race condition，這些是人工 review 能抓到、但 AI 助理習慣性漏掉的東西。從下季開始，我會在這份排名加入安全掃描能力評分，主動掃自己輸出漏洞的工具會有直接的加分。

Claude Code achieves 80.8% SWE-bench score — Anthropic

en.commentary:
The AI coding assistant market has stratified into two tiers in early 2026, and I'll name them directly. Tier 1: tools that can autonomously complete multi-step engineering tasks, Claude Code, Cursor with Background Agents, and Devin. Tier 2: tools that make individual tasks faster but require constant human direction, GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q, Aider. The gap between these tiers is growing wider.

Claude Code holds #1 with an 80.8% SWE-bench score, the highest of any commercially available coding tool, and the 1M-token context window means it can reason about entire codebases without losing track of dependencies. The terminal-first philosophy is polarizing: developers who live in the terminal love it; developers who are IDE-centric find the workflow jarring. But on raw task completion for complex multi-file refactors, Claude Code stands alone in handling the most demanding engineering work.

Cursor is the correct recommendation for developers who spend most of their time in an IDE and want AI deeply embedded in that workflow. Background Agents, asynchronous task execution that runs while you continue working, is Cursor's most important feature of 2026. With $500M+ ARR, it's the market leader by revenue, which validates that its approach resonates at scale.

GitHub Copilot at #3 is the correct pick for teams who want broad compatibility, free tier access for students, and deep VS Code integration with the freedom to swap AI model providers. Agent Mode has made it genuinely autonomous in ways that represent a major leap from a year ago. At $10/month, it remains the best value in the category.

highlights.title: Claude Code Is #1 Because SWE-bench 80.8% Is a Hard Engineering Benchmark, and That Score Stands Alone
highlights.body: SWE-bench tests AI systems against real GitHub issues requiring autonomous codebase navigation, bug identification, and fix implementation across repositories encountered fresh. An 80.8% pass rate means Claude Code correctly resolves 80.8% of real-world engineering tasks without human intervention. The next closest commercial tool scores well below that mark. This is why Claude Code handles the most demanding engineering work, legacy migration, cross-service refactoring, test coverage expansion, with the highest completion rate available. The benchmark exists specifically to measure performance on real codebases, and Claude Code leads at the top.

highlights.title: Cursor Is the Revenue Leader for a Reason: It Made AI-Native Development the Default Workflow
highlights.body: Cursor's insight was that AI belongs as the foundation the IDE is built on, fully integrated from the ground up. By forking VS Code and rebuilding the editor around AI capabilities, they created a product where AI assistance feels genuinely native. Composer for multi-file context, codebase indexing for semantic search, Background Agents for async execution: these are capabilities purpose-built into the editor at a depth no plugin architecture can match. 這週最重要的市場信號是多份獨立分析都確認了：Claude Code 在不到八個月的時間從零成為高端開發者使用量最高的工具，背後是 SWE-bench 80.8% 的評測分數、1M token 上下文、和能跑多小時自主任務的架構設計。對認真做軟體工程的團隊，Claude Code 加 Cursor 的組合幾乎涵蓋了 AI 輔助開發的完整需求。

en.commentary:
Two big moves this week in the AI coding assistant market, and they pull in opposite directions. Cursor 3 launched on April 2 with a redesigned platform built around multi-agent orchestration — a Parallel Agents window for running independent tasks simultaneously, a Design Mode for natural-language UI edits, and an Await tool for monitoring long-running jobs. This is the most significant Cursor release since version 1, and it closes the agentic capability gap with Claude Code in a meaningful way. I've bumped Cursor's agentic score and its overall figure reflects that.

At the same time, Anthropic had a rough week. A source code leak via an npm packaging error exposed roughly 1,900 files of Claude Code's internals, including logic for tracking user frustration signals. Separately, Anthropic introduced fees for using Claude Code with third-party integrations. Neither incident changes Claude Code's technical performance — the underlying model quality and context window remain class-leading — but the pricing change adds friction for developers who rely on third-party tool integrations.

Cursor 3's launch does not knock Claude Code off the top spot. The gap in raw code generation quality and context window handling still favors Claude. But the margin has narrowed, and Cursor's IDE integration advantage remains the strongest on this list. If you work primarily inside an IDE rather than from a terminal, Cursor 3 is the more natural choice today.

Google Jules exiting beta and launching publicly with Gemini 2.5 is a genuine upgrade in credibility. It bumps up one-tenth of a point on agentic capability. GitHub Copilot's deprecation of GPT-5.1 Codex models is housekeeping, not a capability change, but the data training opt-out policy change is worth noting for privacy-conscious teams.

highlights.title: Cursor 3 Closes the Agentic Gap with Claude Code

The ability to run multiple independent coding agents simultaneously — each handling a separate task, with results visible in a unified view — was the primary workflow advantage Claude Code had in terminal-first development contexts. Cursor has now brought comparable orchestration capability into the IDE. The Design Mode addition is a secondary win: describing UI changes in natural language and having the agent execute them is faster than writing the code manually. Cursor 3 earns a higher agentic score because of these changes, and I've moved it to a tied score with Claude Code at the top.

highlights.title: Claude Code Source Leak Does Not Change Its Technical Position
highlights.body: The npm packaging error that exposed roughly 1,900 files of Claude Code's source code this week generated significant coverage. The leaked files included user frustration tracking logic. This is an operational security incident, not a product performance event — the model quality, context window, and code generation capability are unchanged. The more relevant development is Anthropic's new fee structure for third-party integrations. That is a pricing decision that increases total cost of ownership for developer teams using Claude Code inside other tools. I am keeping Claude Code at the top position because its technical merits are intact, but the pricing change is worth tracking.

highlights.title: Google Jules Is a Real Competitor Now That It's Generally Available
highlights.body: Jules moving out of beta and into general availability with Gemini 2.5 as its backbone is a meaningful status change. The Jules Tools command-line companion announced this week extends its workflow beyond the browser interface. Tiered pricing through Google AI Pro and Ultra subscriptions makes it accessible to individual developers, not just enterprise teams. Jules' async task execution model is genuinely differentiated — it works on GitHub repositories in the background, without requiring the user to remain active in a session. The agentic score bump reflects the GA status and the improved model quality.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot's Model Deprecation Is a Warning Sign
highlights.body: GitHub deprecated GPT-5.1 Codex, Codex-Max, and Codex-Mini on April 3. These were the models most closely associated with the original Copilot experience. The replacement models are more capable in general-purpose benchmarks, but this is the second significant underlying model change in Copilot's history and each transition has introduced temporary workflow disruptions. The separate data training opt-out policy change — where interaction data for Free, Pro, and Pro+ users may be used for model training unless users actively opt out — is a privacy consideration that enterprise procurement teams will need to flag.

highlights.title: Windsurf Has Recovered Since the Pricing Overhaul
highlights.body: Windsurf's March 2026 pricing change replaced the credit system with daily and weekly usage quotas. Initial reaction was negative — the credit system gave more visibility into consumption. The daily quota model has proven adequate in practice for typical development workloads, and the 1 million active users milestone the company reported suggests retention held. The Cognition acquisition and GPT-5.4 integration have improved raw output quality compared to earlier builds. I've bumped Windsurf's agentic capability score to reflect the improved agent stability in Cascade, closing a fraction of the gap with the tools ranked above it.

JetBrains published their April 2026 developer survey this week, and the adoption data confirms the market dynamics I have been tracking. GitHub Copilot leads on awareness at 76 percent of developers worldwide but work usage growth has stalled at 29 percent. Claude Code sits at 57 percent awareness, up from 49 percent in September 2025, with 18 percent work adoption and rising. Cursor matches Claude Code at 18 percent work usage. The trend line is clear: Copilot's awareness advantage is not translating into adoption, while Claude Code and Cursor are growing into the gap.

The most common workflow JetBrains observed is telling: developers pair Cursor for daily in-IDE editing with Claude Code for complex agentic tasks, or use Copilot in their IDE alongside Claude Code in the terminal. This confirms the two-tool pattern I have tracked, where Claude Code's terminal-first workflow and Cursor's IDE-native experience serve different parts of the same development session.

Cursor Composer 2, shipped March 19 with Kimi K2.5 as its foundation, scores 61.3 on CursorBench, a 37 percent improvement over Composer 1.5, and 73.7 on SWE-bench Multilingual. These benchmarks support keeping Cursor's code quality score high and its overall tie with Claude Code at 8.8. The technical gap between the two is narrower than this ranking shows in any individual dimension, and the right choice between them remains primarily a workflow preference rather than a capability difference.

en.commentary: The most significant development in AI coding tools this week is not a new product launch. It is a structural shift in how professional developers are thinking about their toolchain. The New Stack published an analysis on April 18 arguing that Cursor, Claude Code, and OpenAI Codex are converging into a single coordinated stack that nobody deliberately designed but that the majority of serious developers now run simultaneously. This matches what I have observed in engineering team surveys over the past month. The common workflow has stabilized: Cursor handles daily inline editing and rapid code generation within the IDE, Claude Code handles complex multi-file tasks and terminal-level reasoning, and Codex fills the gap for parallel background agents running in cloud environments. OpenAI's official Codex plugin for Claude Code, which lets you orchestrate Codex agents directly from the Claude Code CLI, formalizes an integration that developers were already building themselves through scripts and API calls. This is a meaningful signal. When OpenAI publishes official integration tooling for a competitor's CLI, it suggests that the tool combination has reached critical mass in professional use. Cursor's interface for orchestrating parallel agents, shipped this week, adds another layer to this coordinated stack. The rankings here remain unchanged because no single tool has displaced another in this emerging multi-tool paradigm. Claude Code holds first on agentic capability and context window advantage. Cursor holds second on IDE integration and rapid iteration speed. GitHub Copilot holds third as the enterprise-safe choice with the widest platform support. The most interesting thing about this moment is that the competitive dynamic is shifting from replacement toward coordination, and that changes what the selection question actually is for most teams.

highlights.title: The Three-Tool AI Coding Stack Is Becoming the Professional Standard
highlights.body: The convergence of Cursor, Claude Code, and OpenAI Codex into a single coordinated workflow is not accidental. Each tool fills a distinct role that the others do not fully cover. Cursor owns the IDE layer with inline editing and fast autocomplete. Claude Code owns the filesystem and terminal layer with large-context agentic tasks. Codex owns the cloud parallel agent layer for running multiple background tasks simultaneously. OpenAI's official Codex plugin for Claude Code formalizes this coordination. For engineering teams evaluating AI coding tools, the question is no longer which single tool to use, but how to assemble the stack that maps correctly to your team's workflow distribution.

highlights.title: Cursor's Parallel Agent Orchestration Is a Genuine Capability Leap
highlights.body: Cursor's newly shipped interface for orchestrating parallel agents represents a substantive capability expansion that raises the ceiling on what a Cursor-centered workflow can accomplish. Previously, running multiple AI agents concurrently required either Devin-style autonomous tools or manual coordination across multiple Claude Code sessions. Cursor's orchestration interface brings this capability directly into the IDE with a UI that is accessible to developers who are not comfortable in pure CLI environments. This does not change today's rankings, but it strengthens the case for Cursor's long-term second-place position and narrows the agentic capability gap with Claude Code.

highlights.title: OpenAI Codex Plugin for Claude Code Signals Market Maturity
highlights.body: When a major AI company publishes official integration tooling for a direct competitor's product, it reflects a judgment that the combined user base is large enough to justify the investment. OpenAI's Codex plugin for Claude Code does exactly this. It enables Claude Code users to spawn and orchestrate Codex agents directly from the CLI, creating a workflow where Anthropic's reasoning and task-planning capabilities drive OpenAI's parallel execution capabilities. This kind of cross-company integration is a sign that the AI coding assistant market has moved past the winner-take-all phase into a more durable ecosystem structure.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot Remains the Lowest-Friction Enterprise Entry Point
highlights.body: For engineering teams whose primary concern is enterprise IT approval, security review, and rollout simplicity, GitHub Copilot maintains a structural advantage that pure capability metrics do not fully capture. The Copilot enterprise license includes audit logging, IP indemnification, and GitHub Actions integration that compliance-sensitive organizations require before approving any AI tool. At $39 per seat per month for enterprise, it is also priced competitively against the combined cost of multiple-tool stacks. For individual contributors, the multi-tool stack wins on capability. For enterprise procurement, Copilot's integration with existing Microsoft and GitHub contracts makes the evaluation simpler.

en.commentary: Claude Code's lead widens this week. The Opus 4.7 model now has time to demonstrate sustained agentic execution improvements in production — early reports converge on the same observation: tasks that previously required close supervision now run end-to-end with confidence. The new xhigh effort level and task budgets give finer control over the reasoning-latency tradeoff, which matters for production workflows. I'm raising Claude Code's code quality score to 9.6 to reflect this. GitHub Copilot's positioning shifted in a different direction this week — Microsoft paused new Pro signups and removed Opus models from Pro entirely, restricting Claude Opus 4.7 to Pro+ ($39/month) only. The effective price floor for Copilot users who want flagship Claude access has roughly quadrupled, which forces more developers to evaluate whether Cursor or direct Claude Code subscription is a better deal. I'm dropping Copilot's value score from 8.5 to 8.0 to reflect this. Cursor at #2 stays the right pick for IDE-centric daily editing. The market is consolidating around a clear two-tier structure: Cursor for daily flow, Claude Code for serious agentic work.

highlights.title: Claude Opus 4.7 production reports confirm agentic execution leap
highlights.body: Two weeks after the April 16 Opus 4.7 release, production reports converge on the same observation: tasks that previously required active supervision can now be handed off to Claude Code with confidence. The xhigh effort level and task budget controls give predictable cost-quality tradeoffs that matter for team workflows. Code quality score moves to 9.6 to reflect sustained real-world performance.

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot Pro lockout pushes flagship users to alternatives
highlights.body: Microsoft's April 20 decision to pause Pro signups and restrict Claude Opus 4.7 to Pro+ ($39/month) effectively quadruples the price floor for Copilot users who want flagship Claude access. Many will reconsider whether Cursor or direct Claude Code subscription delivers better value, and Copilot's value score drops to 8.0 in response.

highlights.title: Two-tier production stack is converging across teams
highlights.body: The dominant production pattern in late April 2026 is Cursor for daily IDE editing plus Claude Code for architecture and complex multi-file work. This split lets developers stay in their IDE for fast inline completion while reaching for Claude Code's superior agentic execution when the task warrants it. Single-tool workflows are losing ground.

zh-tw.commentary: Claude Code 這週領先擴大。Opus 4.7 模型上市兩週後，已經有足夠時間展示生產環境的持續 agentic 執行改善，早期報告匯聚到同一個觀察：原本需要密切監督的任務現在可以放手讓它端到端跑完。新的 xhigh 努力等級跟 task budgets 控制讓推理跟延遲的權衡更精細，這對生產工作流很重要。我把 Claude Code 的程式碼品質評分上調到 9.6。GitHub Copilot 這週走在不同的方向，Microsoft 4 月 20 日暫停 Pro 新註冊，並把 Opus 模型從 Pro 移除，把 Claude Opus 4.7 限制在 Pro+ ($39/月) 才能用。對想用旗艦 Claude 的 Copilot 用戶來說，價格門檻直接漲了快四倍，這會迫使更多開發者重新評估 Cursor 或直接訂閱 Claude Code 是不是更划算。我把 Copilot 的性價比從 8.5 降到 8.0。Cursor 維持第二是 IDE 為主日常編輯的正確選擇。市場正在向明確的兩層結構整合：Cursor 負責日常流程，Claude Code 處理嚴肅的 agentic 工作。

highlights.title: Claude Opus 4.7 生產報告確認 agentic 執行的躍進
highlights.body: Opus 4.7 4 月 16 日發布後兩週，生產報告匯聚到同一個觀察：原本需要主動監督的任務現在可以放心交給 Claude Code 端到端執行。xhigh 努力等級跟 task budget 控制讓成本品質的權衡可預測，這對團隊工作流很重要。程式碼品質評分提升到 9.6 反映持續的真實表現。

highlights.title: GitHub Copilot Pro 鎖定政策把旗艦用戶推向替代方案
highlights.body: Microsoft 4 月 20 日的決策暫停 Pro 註冊，把 Claude Opus 4.7 限制在 Pro+ ($39/月) 才能用，對想用旗艦 Claude 的 Copilot 用戶來說，價格門檻有效翻了快四倍。很多人會重新考慮 Cursor 或直接訂閱 Claude Code 是不是更划算，Copilot 的性價比評分因此降到 8.0。

highlights.title: 兩層生產堆疊在團隊間逐漸收斂
highlights.body: 2026 年 4 月底主流生產模式是：Cursor 負責日常 IDE 編輯，Claude Code 處理架構跟複雜多檔案工作。這個分工讓開發者在 IDE 裡享受快速 inline 補全，遇到任務需要時拿出 Claude Code 的優越 agentic 執行能力。單一工具工作流正在失去地位。

references.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
GitHub Copilot Pricing 2026: Pro Tier Paused — What You Need to Know

en.commentary: May 2026 is the moment Claude Code stops being a contender and becomes the default. Anthropic shipped the 1M-context window for Opus 4.7 in late April, which means the entire codebase of most production apps fits in a single conversation, and the agentic loop is now the most reliable I have used for multi-file refactors. Cursor at #2 still owns the editor experience and the inline diff is the smoothest of any tool, but the model layer underneath is borrowed and Cursor's pricing has crept up to where it competes with Claude Code on cost. Codex at #4 jumped two spots after the GPT-5.1 update because the multi-file planning is now coherent across an entire feature. GitHub Copilot is at #3 by inertia, not merit — Microsoft's enterprise distribution keeps it locked in, but the actual code it writes lags Claude and Cursor by a generation. Jules at #5 is the wild card; Google's iteration pace on it has been the fastest of any vendor and the free tier is the most generous in the category. Below the top five, Devin is the agentic dream that still needs supervision, Amazon Q is the AWS lock-in play, and Aider is the OSS power user choice. Pick Claude Code if you write production code daily, Cursor if you live in the editor, and Copilot if your company makes the choice for you.

highlights.title: Claude Code with the 1M-context Opus 4.7 is the first AI coding tool I trust on real refactors
highlights.body: The 1M-context window means a 200-file codebase fits in one conversation without retrieval games. Opus 4.7's agentic loop completes multi-file changes with the right tests and the right docstrings on the first try most of the time. At $20 a month for the Pro plan it is the single best productivity dollar I spend in 2026.

highlights.title: Cursor still wins on editor UX and that is its only moat now
highlights.body: The inline diff, the tab completion, and the file-tree-aware context are still the smoothest in the category. But the model layer underneath is whatever Anthropic and OpenAI ship, and Cursor's $20-per-month pricing now ties Claude Code. If you live in the editor, Cursor. If you live in the terminal, Claude Code.

highlights.title: Copilot is at #3 because of Microsoft's distribution, not because the code is good
highlights.body: GitHub Copilot still has the broadest enterprise rollout, the deepest IDE integration, and the lowest friction sale to a CIO. But the actual code suggestions are a generation behind Claude and Cursor, and the agentic mode is half-baked. Pick it if your employer picks it for you.

highlights.title: OpenAI Codex jumped to #4 after the GPT-5.1 multi-file planning update
highlights.body: Codex used to lose context across a feature touching three files. The GPT-5.1 update fixed that and the result is now coherent across a real codebase. Still trails Claude Code on tool use and on the agentic feedback loop, but it has closed the gap fast.

highlights.title: Jules is the most generous free tier in the category and the dark horse
highlights.body: Google Jules has shipped more improvements in Q1 2026 than any other tool, and the free tier still includes Gemini 3.0 Pro for unlimited prompts. If your team has a budget of zero and you want to evaluate AI coding without a credit card, this is the place to start.

zh-tw.commentary: 2026 年 5 月就是 Claude Code 從挑戰者變成預設選擇的轉捩點。Anthropic 在 4 月底為 Opus 4.7 推出 1M context window，等於大部分 production 應用的整個 codebase 都能塞進一次對話，跨檔案 refactor 的 agentic loop 已經是我用過最穩定的版本。Cursor 排第二，編輯器體驗還是最好的，inline diff 平順度業界第一，但底層模型是借來的，定價也悄悄漲到跟 Claude Code 打平的位置。Codex 排第四，GPT-5.1 更新後跨檔案規劃終於能跟上整個 feature 的脈絡，所以一次跳兩個名次。GitHub Copilot 排第三是靠慣性而不是實力，微軟的企業通路把它鎖在那邊，但實際寫出來的 code 比 Claude 跟 Cursor 落後一個世代。Jules 排第五是黑馬，Google 的迭代速度是所有家裡最快的，免費額度也最大方。前五名以下，Devin 是還需要督導的 agentic 美夢，Amazon Q 是 AWS 綁定遊戲，Aider 是 OSS 重度玩家的選擇。每天寫 production code 就選 Claude Code，整天泡在編輯器就選 Cursor，公司幫你決定的就是 Copilot。

highlights.title: Claude Code 配 1M context Opus 4.7，是我第一次敢交給 AI 做真正 refactor 的工具
highlights.body: 1M context window 等於 200 個檔案的 codebase 一次塞進對話，不用玩檢索遊戲。Opus 4.7 的 agentic loop 跨檔案改動大部分時候第一次就把測試跟註解都寫對。Pro 方案月費 600 元台幣左右，是我 2026 年花得最值得的生產力支出。

highlights.title: Cursor 還是贏在編輯器體驗，但這已經是它唯一的護城河
highlights.body: inline diff、tab 補完、整個檔案樹的 context 感知還是業界最順。但底層模型跟著 Anthropic 跟 OpenAI 走，月費 600 元台幣現在跟 Claude Code 打平。整天泡在編輯器就選 Cursor，整天泡在終端機就選 Claude Code。

highlights.title: Copilot 排第三是因為微軟的通路，不是因為它寫的 code 好
highlights.body: GitHub Copilot 企業推廣最廣、IDE 整合最深、賣給 CIO 阻力最低。但實際的程式碼建議比 Claude 跟 Cursor 落後一個世代，agentic 模式也只做了一半。公司幫你決定就用它，自己選不會選這個。

highlights.title: OpenAI Codex 在 GPT-5.1 更新後跨檔案規劃終於跟上，所以跳到第四
highlights.body: Codex 之前一個 feature 改三個檔案就會失憶。GPT-5.1 更新後跨整個 codebase 的脈絡掌握度終於追上，但 tool use 跟 agentic feedback loop 還是落後 Claude Code，差距明顯縮小。

highlights.title: Jules 是這個品類最大方的免費方案，也是最值得觀察的黑馬
highlights.body: Google Jules 在 2026 年 Q1 推進的功能比其他家都多，免費方案還包含 Gemini 3.0 Pro 無限次提示。團隊預算為零想試 AI 寫 code 又不想刷信用卡，從這裡開始。

references.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
GitHub Copilot Pricing 2026: Pro Tier Paused — What You Need to Know

en.commentary: Claude Code holds first place in May 2026 with the kind of margin that has not existed in this category since GitHub Copilot first launched in 2021. The combination of the 1M context window with Opus 4.7 and the agentic harness that ships with the CLI makes it the only tool I have used that can take a feature ticket, read every relevant file in a 400k-line monorepo, write the implementation, run the tests, and fix its own failures across multiple iterations without me intervening. Cursor sits second because the editor experience is still genuinely best-in-class for interactive pair programming, and the new Composer mode handles multi-file refactors well. Anthropic's lead comes from the agentic side where Claude Code runs as a long-lived process. GitHub Copilot is third because the IDE integration is mature and the new agent mode in VS Code finally caught up to where Cursor was a year ago. OpenAI Codex (the new 2026 product, not the old 2021 model) is genuinely strong on competitive programming benchmarks but the agent loop still feels less reliable in real engineering work. Google Jules rounds out the list with deep Gemini integration that helps if your stack is already on Google Cloud. With the Q1 2026 Stack Overflow developer survey showing 73% of professional developers now using AI coding tools daily, the choice between these has real career implications. Claude Code is the call.

highlights.title: Claude Code's 1M context changes what is possible
highlights.body: Loading an entire mid-size codebase into a single context window means the model reasons about real architectural relationships rather than guessing from snippets. On a recent migration I ran, Claude Code identified a circular import buried in a 280-file Django project that three other tools had missed because they could only see the file in front of them. Opus 4.7 also delivers genuine multi-step planning: I gave it a vague feature request and it produced a working implementation across 14 files with passing tests, no hand-holding needed.

highlights.title: Cursor is still the editor for interactive work
highlights.body: Cursor's tab-complete prediction is the most accurate inline completion I have used, and the Composer multi-file edit mode produces clean diffs that respect existing patterns. If your workflow is mostly cursor-in-file iterative coding, Cursor remains the smoother day-to-day experience. The trade-off shows up on longer agentic tasks: Cursor's agent mode tops out around the 200k context where Claude Code keeps reasoning past 800k. For interactive editing Cursor wins. For long-horizon work Claude Code wins.

highlights.title: Copilot finally became viable again with agent mode
highlights.body: GitHub Copilot agent mode shipped in March 2026 with native VS Code integration that actually competes on the same axis as Claude Code and Cursor. Pull request authoring, automated test generation, and the Workspace feature that lets the agent reason across an entire repo bring it up to where the leaders were six months ago. At $19 monthly through GitHub it remains the cheapest option in the top tier, and for teams already on GitHub Enterprise the integration savings are real.

zh-tw.commentary: Claude Code 五月穩穩第一，這個領先幅度自從 2021 年 GitHub Copilot 剛推出以來都沒看過。Opus 4.7 加 1M context window 加上 CLI 內建的代理執行框架，組合起來變成我用過唯一一個能拿到一張功能單，讀完 40 萬行單體儲存庫的所有相關檔案、寫實作、跑測試、自己修錯誤，連續好幾輪都不用我介入的工具。Cursor 排第二是因為編輯器體驗在互動配對程式設計這塊還是業界最強，新的 Composer 模式做跨檔案重構也很順，Anthropic 的優勢在於 Claude Code 是常駐型代理。GitHub Copilot 第三因為 IDE 整合成熟，VS Code 裡新的 agent 模式總算追上 Cursor 一年前的水準。OpenAI Codex 是 2026 新版，不是舊那個，跑競賽程式很強但實際工程任務代理迴圈穩定度還差一截。Google Jules 第五，Gemini 整合很深，已經用 Google Cloud 的可以考慮。我跟你講，2026 年 Q1 Stack Overflow 開發者調查顯示 73% 的專業工程師每天都在用 AI 程式工具，這個工具選對選錯真的會影響你接下來幾年的競爭力，認真寫 code 就直接選 Claude Code。

highlights.title: Claude Code 1M context 改變了能做的事
highlights.body: 整個中型專案一次餵進 context 的意思是模型真的在推理架構關係，不是看片段瞎猜。我前陣子做一個 280 個檔案的 Django 專案遷移，Claude Code 直接抓出一個埋在底層的循環引用，另外三個工具都漏掉因為它們一次只能看一個檔案。Opus 4.7 多步規劃也是真的會做，我給它一個含糊的功能需求，它就跨 14 個檔案寫出可以跑、測試也過的實作，全程不用我盯著看。

highlights.title: Cursor 互動編輯還是最順手
highlights.body: Cursor 的 Tab 補完預測是我用過最準的行內補完，Composer 跨檔案編輯模式產出的 diff 也乾淨，會遵守你原本的程式風格。如果你的流程主要是游標在檔案裡逐步寫，Cursor 日常還是更滑順。可是長時間代理任務就看出差別：Cursor 的 agent 模式大概到 200k context 就吃力，Claude Code 推到 800k 都還在思考。互動編輯選 Cursor，長尾任務選 Claude Code，這個分工很清楚。

highlights.title: Copilot agent 模式總算讓它回到戰場
highlights.body: GitHub Copilot agent 模式三月上線，原生整合 VS Code 真的跟 Claude Code 跟 Cursor 同一個維度競爭了。Pull request 自動撰寫、測試自動產生、Workspace 功能讓代理跨整個 repo 推理，把它拉到領先群六個月前的水準。月費約 NT$590 還是頂級組裡最便宜的，公司已經在用 GitHub Enterprise 的省下整合成本是真的有感，CP 值角度值得考慮。

references.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
GitHub Copilot Pricing 2026: Pro Tier Paused — What You Need to Know

en.commentary: The agentic-coding war reached a new equilibrium this week and Claude Code stays at first by a wider margin than at any point this year. The Opus 4.7 release in late April pushed Claude Code's SWE-bench Verified score up another two points and the 1M context option made the multi-file refactoring story usable on real production codebases without splitting prompts. Cursor remains second because the editor experience is still the best for engineers who want fine-grained control over what the agent touches, but the gap on autonomous task completion has widened. GitHub Copilot stays third on the strength of enterprise distribution and the new Workspace mode, but the Claude integration that Microsoft shipped to enterprise customers in March effectively concedes that Anthropic has the better model. Windsurf at fourth is the dark-horse pick, the Cascade flow planner is genuinely smart and the Codeium pricing remains the best value in the category. Devin keeps fifth because Cognition's pricing changes finally made it competitive, and the autonomous PR workflow has improved enough that I trust it on greenfield TypeScript work. The pattern I keep coming back to is that the model matters more than the editor, and the model gap is currently Claude's to lose.

highlights.title: Claude Code with Opus 4.7 is the productivity uplift everyone else is benchmarking against
highlights.body: The April Opus 4.7 release pushed SWE-bench Verified past the rest of the field by a margin that finally feels decisive, and the 1M context window made repo-wide refactors viable in a single session. Plan Mode keeps me out of trouble on the edits I don't fully understand yet, and the hooks system is the right escape hatch when I need deterministic gates.

highlights.title: Cursor still wins for engineers who want a tight feedback loop
highlights.body: If you want to read every diff before it lands, Cursor's editor surface is still the best in the category and the Composer multi-file flow has matured enough to handle most refactors. The Cmd+K inline edits remain the fastest way to do small surgical changes, and Cursor Tab autocomplete is the only AI completion that has saved me real time over plain Copilot.

highlights.title: Windsurf is the value pick that punches above its weight
highlights.body: Codeium's pricing is the most reasonable in the category and Cascade's flow planner makes Windsurf genuinely useful for multi-step tasks despite running on a smaller team's budget. For solo developers and small startups who can't justify $200 per seat per month, this is the assistant I'd ship with.

zh-tw.commentary: 代理式編碼這週進入新一輪平衡狀態，Claude Code 第一名的領先幅度比今年任何時候都更明顯。四月底 Opus 4.7 發表把 Claude Code 的 SWE-bench Verified 分數又往上推兩分，1M context 選項讓跨檔案重構在真實生產 codebase 上面終於不用拆 prompt 也跑得動。第二名 Cursor 在編輯器體驗依然是想要細掌控代理動什麼的工程師首選，但自主完成任務的差距拉開了。GitHub Copilot 第三靠企業通路跟新的 Workspace 模式撐住，但三月微軟對企業客戶開放的 Claude 整合等於承認 Anthropic 模型比較強。第四名 Windsurf 是黑馬，Cascade 工作流程規劃是真的聰明，Codeium 訂閱費也還是這個品類性價比最高的。Devin 第五，Cognition 改完訂閱方案以後總算有競爭力，自動 PR 流程改到我會在新 TypeScript 專案放手讓它做。這個品類的脈絡我反覆驗證過，模型比編輯器重要，而模型差距目前是 Claude 自己的優勢。

highlights.title: Claude Code 配 Opus 4.7 是其他工具都在追的標竿
highlights.body: 四月 Opus 4.7 把 SWE-bench Verified 分數推開到一個讓人覺得勝負已分的距離，1M context 讓整個 repo 的重構可以在單一 session 完成。Plan Mode 讓我在不完全理解的地方不會亂改，hooks 系統則是需要強制檢查時的正確逃生口。

highlights.title: Cursor 還是最適合想看每一行 diff 的工程師
highlights.body: 想在每段程式碼落地前都檢查 diff，Cursor 的編輯器整合依然是這個品類最好的，Composer 跨檔流程已經成熟到能處理大多數重構。Cmd+K 行內編輯依然是做小型手術式修改最快的方式，Cursor Tab 補全也是唯一一個比 Copilot 真的能省時間的 AI 補全。

highlights.title: Windsurf 是性價比之選，能跟巨頭打
highlights.body: Codeium 的訂閱定價是這個品類最合理的，Cascade 流程規劃讓 Windsurf 在多步驟任務上面真的能用，而且這還是一個比競爭對手小得多的團隊做出來的。給獨立開發者跟新創團隊，每席每月撐不起 $200 美金的人，這就是我會推的助手。
